session.user:
▐▛███▜▌ Claude Code v2.1.70 ▝▜█████▛▘ Opus 4.6 · Claude Max ▘▘ ▝▝ /workspaces/erk
❯ /exit ⎿ Catch you later!
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ▪▪▪ ─ ❯ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
Resume this session with: claude --resume 2ae49e29-296e-4c06-9518-83ef40ec3963 Connection to localhost closed. (erk) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) erk down (erk) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) erk-cft zsh: command not found: erk-cft (erk) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) erk-activate -> cd /Users/schrockn/code/erk -> Activating venv: /Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv (3.13.1) Activated: /Users/schrockn/code/erk (erk) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) pwd /Users/schrockn/code/erk (erk) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) erk wt create -h Usage: erk wt create [OPTIONS] NAME
Create a worktree and write a .env file.
Reads config.toml for env templates and post-create commands (if present). If --from-plan-file is provided, derives name from the plan filename and creates an implementation context folder in the worktree. If --from-plan is provided, fetches the plan, validates the erk-plan label, derives name from the plan title, and creates an impl folder with plan-ref.json metadata. If --from-current-branch is provided, moves the current branch to the new worktree. If --from-branch is provided, creates a worktree from an existing branch.
By default, the command checks if a branch with the same name already exists on the 'origin' remote. If a conflict is detected, the command fails with an error. Use --skip-remote-check to bypass this validation for offline workflows.
Options: --branch TEXT Branch name to create and check out in the worktree. Defaults to NAME if omitted. --ref TEXT Git ref to base the worktree on (e.g. HEAD, origin/main). Defaults to HEAD if omitted. --no-post Skip running post-create commands from config.toml. --from-plan-file FILE Path to a plan markdown file. Will derive worktree name from filename and create implementation context folder with plan.md in the worktree. Worktree names are automatically suffixed with the current date (-YY-MM-DD) and versioned if duplicates exist. --keep-plan-file Copy the plan file instead of moving it (requires --from-plan-file). --from-plan TEXT Plan number or URL with erk-plan label. Fetches plan content and creates worktree with impl folder and plan-ref.json metadata. Worktree names are automatically suffixed with the current date (-YY-MM- DD) and versioned if duplicates exist. --copy-plan Copy .erk/impl-context/ directory from current worktree to new worktree. Useful for multi-phase workflows where each phase builds on the previous plan. Mutually exclusive with --from-plan. --from-current-branch Move the current branch to the new worktree, then switch current worktree to --ref (defaults to main/master). NAME defaults to current branch name. --from-branch TEXT Create worktree from an existing branch. NAME defaults to the branch name. --json Output JSON with worktree information instead of human-readable messages. --stay Stay in current directory instead of switching to new worktree. --skip-remote-check Skip checking if branch exists on remote (for offline work) -h, --help Show this message and exit.
Hidden Options: --script Output shell script for integration. NOT a dry run. (erk) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) erk slot assign -h Usage: erk slot assign [OPTIONS] BRANCH
Assign an EXISTING branch to an available pool slot.
BRANCH is the name of an existing git branch to assign to the pool.
The command will: 1. Verify the branch EXISTS (fails if it doesn't) 2. Find the next available slot in the pool 3. Create a worktree for that slot if needed 4. Assign the branch to the slot 5. Persist the assignment to pool.json
Use `erk branch create` to create a NEW branch and assign it.
Options: -f, --force Auto-unassign oldest branch if pool is full -h, --help Show this message and exit. The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

cli() ~~~^^ File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1485, in __call__ return self.main(*args, **kwargs) ~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File self.git.branch.checkout_branch(repo_root, branch) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/real.py", line 87, in checkout_branch run_subprocess_with_context( ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^ cmd=["git", "checkout", branch], ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ operation_context=f"checkout branch '{branch}'", ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ cwd=cwd, ^^^^^^^^ ) ^ File "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/subprocess_utils.py", line 162, in run_subprocess_with_context raise RuntimeError(error_msg) from e RuntimeError: Failed to checkout branch 'plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330' Command: git checkout plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330 Exit code: 128 stderr: fatal: 'plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330' is already used by worktree at '/Users/schrockn/code/erk' (erk) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330)
i want a command that can take an existing branch and move it to a slow. similar to erk wt create --from-current-branch but assigning to slow instead of creating a new wt
session.assistant: Let me explore the slot system and the `--from-current-branch` logic to understand what's needed.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: Explore slot assign and wt create

FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 702 chars]...to a pool slot (similar to --from-current-branch but for slots instead of new worktrees).

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:
## Summary
Based on my exploration of the erk codebase, here's what I found:
### 1. **`erk slot assign` Command and `allocate_slot_for_branch` Function**
**Location:** `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/slot/assign_cmd.py` and `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/slot/common.py`
**How `erk slot assign` works:** - Takes an existing branch name as argument - Calls `allocate_slot_for_branch()` with `reuse_inactive_slots=True` and `cleanup_artifacts=True` - If the result shows `already_assigned=True`, the command returns an error with exit code 1 - Otherwise, displays success message
**How `allocate_slot_for_branch()` works (lines 566-730 in common.py):**
The function implements a unified slot allocation algorithm with these steps:
1. **Load pool state:** Reads `pool.json` or creates a fresh state 2. **Sync assignments:** Calls `sync_pool_assignments()` to reconcile recorded state with actual git state 3. **Check if branch already assigned:** If yes, validates and returns early with `already_assigned=True` 4. **Prefer reusing unassigned worktrees:** Calls `find_inactive_slot()` to find an existing (but unassigned) slot that's clean 5. **Fall back to on-demand creation:** If no inactive slot available, finds next available slot number via `find_next_available_slot()` 6. **Handle pool full:** If all slots are used, prompts user or auto-unassigns oldest if `--force` given 7. **Create worktree:** Either reuses existing worktree or creates new one 8. **Cleanup artifacts:** Removes `.erk/impl-context/` and `.erk/scratch/` if `cleanup_artifacts=True` 9. **Checkout branch:** Uses `ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch()` to check out the branch 10. **Create assignment:** Adds new `SlotAssignment` to pool state and saves to `pool.json`
... [109 lines omitted] ...
3. **Error handling:** The current worktree becomes detached or on a different branch - this should be clearly communicated to the user
session.assistant: Good exploration. Let me read the key files to finalize the plan.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Slot assign command - assign an existing branch to a worktree slot.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.commands.slot.common import allocate_slot_for_branch 6→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 7→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 8→from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir 9→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 10→ 11→ 12→@click.command("assign") 13→@click.argument("branch_name", metavar="BRANCH") 14→@click.option("-f", "--force", is_flag=True, help="Auto-unassign oldest branch if pool is full") 15→@click.pass_obj 16→def slot_assign(ctx: ErkContext, branch_name: str, force: bool) -> None: 17→ """Assign an EXISTING branch to an available pool slot. 18→ 19→ BRANCH is the name of an existing git branch to assign to the pool. 20→ 21→ The command will: 22→ 1. Verify the branch EXISTS (fails if it doesn't) 23→ 2. Find the next available slot in the pool 24→ 3. Create a worktree for that slot if needed 25→ 4. Assign the branch to the slot 26→ 5. Persist the assignment to pool.json 27→ 28→ Use `erk branch create` to create a NEW branch and assign it. 29→ """ 30→ repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
... [29 lines omitted] ...
37→ f"Error: Branch '{branch_name}' does not exist.\n" 53→ # If branch was already assigned, report error (assign command expects unassigned branch) 55→ user_output(f"Error: Branch '{branch_name}' already assigned to {result.slot_name}")

1→"""Slot infrastructure management commands.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.alias import register_with_aliases 6→from erk.cli.commands.slot.assign_cmd import slot_assign 7→from erk.cli.commands.slot.init_pool_cmd import slot_init_pool 8→from erk.cli.commands.slot.list_cmd import slot_list 9→from erk.cli.commands.slot.repair_cmd import slot_repair 10→from erk.cli.commands.slot.unassign_cmd import slot_unassign 11→from erk.cli.help_formatter import ErkCommandGroup 12→ 13→ 14→@click.group("slot", cls=ErkCommandGroup, grouped=False) 15→def slot_group() -> None: 16→ """Manage worktree pool slots.""" 17→ pass 18→ 19→ 20→# Register subcommands 21→slot_group.add_command(slot_assign) 22→slot_group.add_command(slot_init_pool) 23→slot_group.add_command(slot_repair) 24→slot_group.add_command(slot_unassign) 25→register_with_aliases(slot_group, slot_list) 26→

750→ worktree_name=name, 751→ worktree_path=wt_path, 752→ branch_name=existing_branch, 753→ plan_file_path=plan_path, 754→ status="exists", 755→ ) 756→ user_output(json_response) 757→ raise SystemExit(1) from None 758→ Ensure.invariant(False, f"Worktree path already exists: {wt_path}") 759→ 760→ # Handle from-current-branch logic: switch current worktree first 761→ to_branch = None 762→ if from_current_branch: 763→ current_branch = Ensure.not_none( 764→ ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.cwd), "Unable to determine current branch" 765→ ) 766→ 767→ # Determine preferred branch to checkout (prioritize Graphite parent) 768→ parent_branch = ( 769→ ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo.root, current_branch) 770→ if current_branch 771→ else None 772→ ) 773→ 774→ if parent_branch: 775→ # Prefer Graphite parent branch 776→ to_branch = parent_branch 777→ elif ref: 778→ # Use ref if provided 779→ to_branch = ref
... [50 lines omitted] ...

560→ slot_name=current_assignment.slot_name, 561→ worktree_path=current_assignment.worktree_path, 562→ already_assigned=True, 563→ ) 564→ 565→ 566→def allocate_slot_for_branch( 567→ ctx: ErkContext, 568→ repo: RepoContext, 569→ branch_name: str, 570→ *, 571→ force: bool, 572→ reuse_inactive_slots: bool, 573→ cleanup_artifacts: bool, 574→) -> SlotAllocationResult: 575→ """Allocate a pool slot for a branch and setup the worktree. 576→ 577→ This is the unified slot allocation algorithm used by all commands 578→ that need to assign branches to pool slots. 579→ 580→ The branch MUST already exist before calling this function. 581→ 582→ Args: 583→ ctx: Erk context (uses ctx.time.now() for testable timestamps) 584→ repo: Repository context with pool_json_path and worktrees_dir 585→ branch_name: Name of existing branch to assign 586→ force: Auto-unassign oldest if pool is full (no interactive prompt) 587→ reuse_inactive_slots: Try to reuse unassigned worktrees first 588→ cleanup_artifacts: Remove .erk/impl-context/ and .erk/scratch/ on worktree reuse 589→

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/misty-questing-harp.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Add `--from-current-branch` flag to `erk slot assign` Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
---
# Plan: Add `--from-current-branch` flag to `erk slot assign`
## Context
`erk slot assign <branch>` fails when the branch is already checked out in the current worktree because git doesn't allow the same branch to be checked out in multiple worktrees. The user wants a `--from-current-branch` flag (mirroring `erk wt create --from-current-branch`) that moves the current branch out of the current worktree and into a pool slot.
## Files to Modify
1. **`src/erk/cli/commands/slot/assign_cmd.py`** — Add `--from-current-branch` flag 2. **`tests/`** — Add/update tests for the new flag
## Implementation
### 1. Modify `assign_cmd.py`
Add `--from-current-branch` click option (is_flag=True). When set:
- Ignore the `BRANCH` argument (make it optional when flag is set) - Get current branch via `ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.cwd)` - Determine target branch to switch to (reuse logic from `wt/create_cmd.py:767-802`): 1. Prefer Graphite parent branch 2. Fall back to trunk branch (main/master) - Validate current_branch != to_branch - Switch current worktree: check if target is available, checkout or detach - Call `allocate_slot_for_branch()` with the now-free branch
Make `BRANCH` argument optional (required=False) and validate that exactly one of BRANCH or `--from-current-branch` is provided.
### 2. Branch-switching logic
The pattern (~10 lines) from `wt/create_cmd.py:760-814`:
```python current_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.cwd) parent_branch = ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo.root, current_branch) to_branch = parent_branch or ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
Ensure.invariant(current_branch != to_branch, ...)
checkout_path = ctx.git.worktree.is_branch_checked_out(repo.root, to_branch) if checkout_path is not None: ctx.branch_manager.checkout_detached(ctx.cwd, current_branch) else: ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(ctx.cwd, to_branch) ```
### 3. Tests
- `--from-current-branch` moves branch to slot and switches current worktree - Error when already on trunk branch - Error when providing both BRANCH arg and `--from-current-branch`
## Verification
- Run existing slot assign tests to confirm no regressions - Run `make fast-ci` for full validation
---

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'What would you like to do with this plan?', 'header': 'br:master', 'options...[truncated 313 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Let me generate the branch slug and summary, then save the plan.
**Plan title:** "Add `--from-current-branch` flag to `erk slot assign`" **Branch slug:** `add-slot-assign-from-branch` **Summary:** Adds a --from-current-branch flag to erk slot assign that moves the current branch out of the current worktree and into a pool slot. This mirrors the existing --from-current-branch behavior on erk wt create.

tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" --bra...[truncated 148 chars]...o a pool slot. This mirrors the existing --from-current-branch behavior on erk wt create."
Save plan as draft PR via erk exec plan-save

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8865 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement plan #8865: In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout plan #8865: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8865 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8865
Dispatch plan #8865: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8865 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
Session complete. Do NOT call ExitPlanMode again.
session.assistant:
Plan #8865 saved successfully. Here are next steps:
**Implement plan #8865:** ``` In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8865 --script)" && erk implement -d ```
**Checkout plan #8865:** ``` In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8865 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8865 ```
**Dispatch plan #8865:** ``` CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8865 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch ```
session.user:
# /erk:pr-dispatch
## Goal
Find the most recent plan created in this conversation and dispatch it for remote AI implementation via `erk pr dispatch`.
## What This Command Does
1. Search conversation for the last plan reference 2. Extract the plan number 3. Run `erk pr dispatch <plan_number>` to trigger remote implementation
## Finding the Plan
Search the conversation from bottom to top for these patterns (in priority order):
1. **Draft PR reference**: `saved as draft PR #<number>` or `draft PR #<number>` 2. **Pull request URL**: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<number>` 3. **Plan URL**: `https://github.com/.../pull/<number>` (legacy: `https://github.com/.../issues/<number>`)
Extract the plan number from the most recent match.
## Execution
Once you have the plan number, run:
```bash erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session:
```bash erk exec marker delete --session-id "dccb6a2a-901b-4930-b39e-11aabce6c96c" plan-saved-issue ```
Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state).
## Error Cases
- **No plan found in conversation**: Report "No plan found in conversation. Run /erk:plan-save first to create a plan." - **erk pr dispatch fails**: Display the error output from the command (erk pr dispatch validates the plan)
session.assistant: The most recent plan is #8865. Dispatching it now.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Dispatch plan #8865 for remote AI implementation

✓ All 1 plan(s) validated

Dispatching PR #8865...
Fetching plan content... Syncing branch: plnd/add-slot-assign-from-branc-03-06-1435 Committing plan to branch... ✓ Branch pushed to remote
Dispatching workflow: plan-implement.yml Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow dispatched. ✓ Dispatch metadata written Posting queued event comment... ✓ Queued event comment posted
✓ 1 plan(s) dispatched successfully!
Dispatched plans:
... [4 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Clear plan-saved marker to allow new plans

session.assistant: Plan #8865 dispatched for remote implementation. The workflow is running at the link above.
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
